refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of load→travel→dump cycle time performance against customer-defined targets.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure and report cycle time data, and (2) the operational achievement of a target—the latter is a performance outcome dependent on multiple system and operational factors beyond a single design parameter. By refining to focus on the measurement and reporting capability, the FR becomes solution-neutral (avoids prescribing how cycle time is optimized) and atomic (single, testable obligation). Achievement of the cycle time target is an acceptance criterion validated through operational testing, not a functional requirement of the system design itself.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution captures and reports cycle time metrics (load start → travel start → dump start → dump end timestamps) with ≤5% measurement error under representative duty cycles. Validate that reported p95 cycle time can be compared against customer-defined CT_target.
sub_frs: Capture cycle phase transition events (load initiation, travel initiation, dump initiation, dump completion) with timestamp and location data.
Aggregate and compute cycle time statistics (mean, p95, p99) from captured events.
Report cycle time performance data to Site Manager and System Engineer with sufficient granularity to diagnose deviations from CT_target.
refined_fr.note: The refined FR is solution-neutral but decomposes into three atomic sub-FRs, each addressing a distinct functional obligation in the measurement and reporting pipeline. The acceptance criterion (p95 ≤ CT_target under duty cycle) remains a performance validation gate, not a functional requirement.

candidate_dps: DP1: Instrument load-bearing structure and dump mechanism with displacement/force transducers; aggregate raw signals into a time-series event log; apply threshold-crossing logic to detect cycle phase boundaries (load pickup, travel start, dump completion); compute elapsed time between phase transitions; compare p95 aggregate against CT_target via statistical analysis module.

DP2: Deploy discrete event sensors (binary switches/proximity detectors) at fixed cycle checkpoints (load engaged, vehicle departed, dump gate opened, dump gate closed); timestamp each event; reconstruct cycle duration from event sequence; filter outliers and compute p95 percentile; report pass/fail against CT_target.

DP3: Integrate continuous position/velocity telemetry from vehicle propulsion and hydraulic actuation systems; fuse multi-source signals into a unified motion model; apply state-machine logic to infer cycle phases; extract phase transition times; aggregate p95 metric and compare to CT_target with confidence bounds.

DP4: Capture operator-initiated cycle start/end markers (manual logging or button press) combined with autonomous phase detection (load weight threshold, dump mechanism position); reconcile manual and automatic timestamps; compute cycle time as difference; report statistical summary (p95, mean, std dev) against CT_target with audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Instrument load-bearing structure and dump mechanism with displacement/force transducers; aggregate raw signals into a time-series event log; apply threshold-crossing logic to detect cycle phase boundaries (load pickup, travel start, dump completion); compute elapsed time between phase transitions; compare p95 aggregate against CT_target via statistical analysis module.

potential_sources: Signal conditioning and transducer selection are decoupled from phase-detection logic (threshold values can be tuned independently).
Event log aggregation is independent of downstream statistical analysis (p95 computation can be replaced without affecting data collection).
Threshold-crossing detection is solution-neutral (does not mandate specific sensor technology or communication protocol).

Risk: Transducer placement and calibration must be coordinated with mechanical design; if not managed, sensor drift or mounting failures could invalidate measurements. Mitigation: establish sensor validation and recalibration procedures as part of operational policy.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 exhibits low functional coupling because signal acquisition, event detection, and metric computation are logically separable. The main coupling point is the dependency on accurate transducer calibration and stable mounting—a physical/operational concern, not a logical design flaw. DP2 (discrete sensors) is simpler but less robust to missed events; DP3 (continuous fusion) is more complex and introduces state-estimation risk; DP4 (hybrid manual/auto) introduces operator variability and audit complexity. DP1 balances robustness, observability, and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Transducers can be mounted on load-bearing structure and dump mechanism without compromising structural integrity or safety. Risk: Mounting points may not exist or may require design modification. Mitigation: early mechanical design review.

Assumption: Threshold values for phase detection (e.g., load pickup force, dump gate position) can be reliably established and remain stable across duty cycles and site conditions. Risk: Threshold drift due to wear, temperature, or load variability. Mitigation: periodic recalibration protocol and adaptive threshold logic.

Assumption: Raw signal noise and transducer drift do not obscure phase transitions. Risk: False positives/negatives in event detection under high-vibration or extreme-temperature conditions. Mitigation: signal filtering, redundant sensors, or confidence scoring.

Assumption: Time-series event log can be reliably transmitted and stored (no data loss or corruption). Risk: Communication failures or storage saturation. Mitigation: local buffering, checksums, and data validation.

Assumption: p95 percentile computation is statistically valid over the agreed duty cycle sample size. Risk: Insufficient samples or non-stationary cycle times may invalidate confidence in p95 estimate. Mitigation: define minimum sample size and re-baseline if operational conditions change.

Assumption: Customer-defined CT_target is achievable under stated site conditions and worksite safety policies. Risk: Target may be infeasible, leading to chronic non-compliance. Mitigation: validate target feasibility during acceptance criteria negotiation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR (refined): Enable measurement and reporting of load→travel→dump cycle time performance (p95 percentile) against customer-defined targets, with automated detection of cycle phase boundaries, statistical aggregation, and audit-trail logging to support continuous improvement and compliance verification under agreed duty cycle and site conditions. DP (refined): Instrument load-bearing structure and dump mechanism with displacement/force transducers; condition and log raw signals as time-series events; apply calibrated threshold-crossing logic to detect cycle phase boundaries (load pickup, travel initiation, dump completion); compute cycle duration as elapsed time between phase transitions; aggregate p95 percentile over a rolling or batch window; compare against CT_target and report pass/fail status with confidence bounds and data quality metrics (sample count, outlier count, sensor health). Introduce explicit IM layer: (IM1) Transducer and signal conditioning module (owned by mechanical/electrical engineering), (IM2) Event detection and logging module (owned by controls/firmware), (IM3) Statistical analysis and reporting module (owned by data/analytics team). This separation enables independent testing, calibration, and evolution of each layer.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Integrated load-bearing instrumentation, signal aggregation, cycle-phase detection, temporal analysis, and statistical comparison against cycle-time targets. Encompasses transducer integration, event logging, threshold logic, phase-transition timing, and p95 aggregate evaluation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Raw transducer signals (displacement, force) streamed from instrumented structure; time-series event log of detected phase boundaries; computed cycle metrics (phase durations, p95 aggregate cycle time).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: CT_target specification and statistical analysis parameters (e.g., percentile threshold, acceptable variance); configuration of phase-detection thresholds and transducer calibration.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Alerts triggered when p95 aggregate cycle time exceeds CT_target or transducer signal anomalies detected (e.g., sensor failure, out-of-range readings).

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment. NGHE Solution is the natural home for load-bearing instrumentation, signal processing, and cycle-time analytics. Transducer integration, event logging, threshold logic, and statistical analysis are all core to NGHE's mission of autonomous load handling. Minor diffusion: CT_target tasking and safety alerts must cross module boundaries via interfaces, but this is expected and well-managed.

Poor containment. Vehicle module is responsible for platform dynamics and motion control, not load instrumentation or cycle analytics. Placing DP here would scatter signal processing, event logging, and statistical analysis across Vehicle and NGHE, creating tight coupling and duplicated logic. High diffusion risk.

Very poor containment. Teleoperation handles remote operator interface and command relay, not autonomous load-bearing instrumentation or analytics. DP would be fundamentally misaligned with Teleoperation's scope. Severe diffusion: all transducer integration, event logging, and statistical analysis would leak into NGHE Solution.

Weak containment. Safety Services is responsible for hazard detection and alert generation, not instrumentation or cycle-time analytics. While SafetyAlert output belongs in Safety Services, the upstream signal processing, event logging, and statistical comparison logic would diffuse into NGHE Solution. Splitting DP across modules violates cohesion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP is a cohesive, autonomous load-handling analytics capability that logically belongs within NGHE Solution. Transducer instrumentation, signal aggregation, phase-detection logic, temporal analysis, and statistical comparison are all intrinsic to NGHE's function of autonomous cycle execution and performance monitoring. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) provide clean boundaries for data flow and configuration without requiring DP logic to diffuse across module lines.
containment_risks: Transducer calibration and hardware integration may require Vehicle-level coordination if sensors are mounted on vehicle chassis; mitigate by defining a clear sensor-mounting interface between NGHE Solution and Vehicle.
Real-time signal processing latency could impact cycle-phase detection accuracy; ensure NGHE Solution has sufficient computational resources and prioritization to meet timing requirements.
CT_target and statistical thresholds may need dynamic adjustment based on operational context (terrain, load type); ensure Tasking interface supports parameterization without requiring code changes.
Safety alerts triggered by cycle-time exceedance must be coordinated with Vehicle motion control; SafetyAlert interface must guarantee timely delivery to Safety Services and Vehicle.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criterion 'p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions' is directly measurable via the DP's statistical analysis module. Raw transducer signals and computed cycle metrics flow through Telemetry interface, enabling independent validation. Phase-transition detection logic is deterministic and auditable. Worksite safety plan integration is addressed via SafetyAlert interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. NGHE Solution is the unambiguous owner of load-bearing instrumentation, signal aggregation, phase detection, and temporal analysis. Ownership boundary is well-defined; external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) establish clean data flow without functional ambiguity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP is functionally complete and logically sound. It addresses all aspects of the FR: (1) instrumentation captures load/travel/dump events; (2) threshold-crossing logic detects phase boundaries; (3) temporal analysis computes cycle time; (4) statistical comparison validates p95 against CT_target. DP is expressed at appropriate abstraction level for design domain (logical solution) without prescribing implementation details prematurely.

diffusion_risks: Transducer calibration and hardware mounting: If sensor integration requires Vehicle-level coordination (e.g., mounting brackets, wiring harnesses on vehicle chassis), a clear sensor-mounting interface must be defined between NGHE Solution and Vehicle to prevent signal-processing logic from diffusing into Vehicle module. Recommend: NGHE Solution owns all signal conditioning and aggregation; Vehicle owns only physical sensor placement and power delivery.

Real-time signal processing latency: Phase-detection accuracy depends on timely transducer sampling and threshold evaluation. If NGHE Solution lacks sufficient computational priority or real-time guarantees, cycle-phase detection may drift, invalidating cycle-time measurements. Recommend: Establish latency SLA (e.g., <100 ms phase-detection latency) and allocate dedicated compute resources within NGHE Solution.

Dynamic CT_target and threshold adjustment: If operational context (terrain, load type, weather) requires frequent re-parameterization of CT_target or phase-detection thresholds, the Tasking interface must support runtime configuration without code changes. Risk: Ad-hoc threshold tuning could leak into Vehicle or Safety Services if not properly encapsulated. Recommend: Tasking interface provides parameterized threshold updates; NGHE Solution applies them without external logic.

SafetyAlert coordination with Vehicle motion control: Alerts triggered by cycle-time exceedance must reach Safety Services and Vehicle in time to halt unsafe motion. If SafetyAlert latency is high or Vehicle does not respond promptly, safety plan compliance is at risk. Recommend: SafetyAlert interface guarantees <50 ms delivery; Vehicle has hard real-time handler for motion-halt commands.

Statistical analysis module scope: The DP mentions 'statistical analysis module' but does not specify whether this is a standalone sub-module within NGHE Solution or distributed across signal processing and reporting. Risk: If statistical logic is scattered across multiple code files or services, DP<->IM bridge is weak. Recommend: Encapsulate all p95 computation, variance analysis, and CT_target comparison in a single, cohesive statistical analysis sub-module owned by NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). NGHE Solution is the natural and only appropriate home for this DP. Transducer instrumentation, signal aggregation, phase-detection logic, temporal analysis, and statistical comparison are all intrinsic to autonomous load-handling performance monitoring. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are correctly excluded from DP ownership. External interfaces (Telemetry out, Tasking in, SafetyAlert out) are well-defined and do not require DP logic to diffuse. Minor diffusion risks are boundary-coordination issues (sensor mounting, latency, parameterization, alert delivery), not fundamental architectural misalignment. All risks are mitigable via interface discipline and resource allocation.

recommended_revisions: Refine Tasking interface specification: Define the exact schema and update frequency for CT_target and phase-detection threshold parameters. Specify whether updates are applied immediately, at cycle boundaries, or with a grace period. Ensure NGHE Solution can validate incoming parameters before applying them.

Establish latency SLA for phase-detection and SafetyAlert: Add explicit timing requirements (e.g., phase-detection latency <100 ms, SafetyAlert delivery <50 ms) to the DP and IM boundary specification. Allocate real-time compute resources within NGHE Solution to meet these SLAs.

Define sensor-mounting interface between NGHE Solution and Vehicle: Specify which party owns transducer placement, wiring, power delivery, and mechanical integration. NGHE Solution should own all signal conditioning and aggregation; Vehicle should own only physical mounting and power. Document this boundary clearly to prevent diffusion.

Encapsulate statistical analysis as a dedicated sub-module: Ensure all p95 computation, variance analysis, and CT_target comparison logic is contained in a single, cohesive statistical analysis sub-module within NGHE Solution. Avoid scattering statistical logic across multiple code files or services.

Add acceptance test plan: Define how p95 ≤ CT_target will be verified under agreed duty cycle and site conditions. Specify test duration, sample size, environmental conditions, and acceptance thresholds. Ensure Telemetry interface provides sufficient data granularity for independent validation.

Coordinate SafetyAlert with Safety Services and Vehicle: Ensure SafetyAlert interface includes hard real-time guarantees and Vehicle motion-halt handlers. Document the safety plan integration and verify that alerts trigger appropriate protective actions within acceptable latency.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence and excellent containment (0.92). The DP is functionally complete, logically sound, and appropriately scoped to NGHE Solution. However, the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks reveal that several critical sub-concerns—particularly real-time latency guarantees, statistical analysis encapsulation, sensor-mounting interface discipline, and SafetyAlert coordination—are not yet resolved at the current level of abstraction. Given the high safety criticality of cycle-time validation and the explicit mention of a 'statistical analysis module' and multiple interface SLAs in the DP, decomposition into next-level sub-FRs is warranted to ensure these concerns are properly owned, specified, and verified. Stopping here would leave architectural and timing risks unresolved; revising the current level would be redundant given that the DP is already sound. Decomposition allows each sub-concern to be treated as a distinct FR with its own DP-IM mapping and acceptance criteria.
next_level_sub_frs: FR 2.1: Acquire and condition transducer signals (load, travel, dump) with sufficient fidelity and latency to enable deterministic phase-detection logic.
FR 2.2: Detect phase boundaries (load-start, travel-start, dump-start, dump-end) in real time with latency <100 ms and deterministic accuracy under agreed duty cycles.
FR 2.3: Compute cycle time from phase-boundary timestamps and aggregate cycle-time samples into a rolling statistical window.
FR 2.4: Compute p95 cycle time from the statistical window and compare against CT_target to determine compliance status.
FR 2.5: Encapsulate all statistical analysis (p95 computation, variance tracking, CT_target comparison) in a dedicated, cohesive sub-module to prevent logic diffusion.
FR 2.6: Accept parameterized CT_target and phase-detection threshold updates from Tasking interface without code changes, with validation and atomic application.
FR 2.7: Deliver cycle-time exceedance alerts to SafetyAlert interface with latency <50 ms and guaranteed delivery under agreed duty cycles.
FR 2.8: Define and enforce sensor-mounting and power-delivery interface with Vehicle to prevent signal-processing logic from diffusing into Vehicle module.

revise.why: No revision of current-level FR, DP, or IM is necessary. All three gates pass; the DP is sound and well-scoped. Decomposition is the appropriate next step.

assumptions: NGHE Solution has access to real-time compute resources (CPU, memory, scheduling priority) sufficient to meet <100 ms phase-detection latency and <50 ms SafetyAlert delivery SLAs.
Transducer hardware (load cells, position sensors, dump-event detectors) is available and can be integrated into Vehicle with a clean mounting and power interface.
Tasking interface can provide CT_target and threshold parameters at runtime with sufficient frequency and granularity to support operational context changes (terrain, load type, weather).
SafetyAlert interface is connected to Safety Services and Vehicle motion-control systems with hard real-time guarantees and documented handlers for motion-halt commands.
Telemetry interface provides sufficient data granularity (raw transducer signals, computed cycle metrics, phase timestamps) to enable independent validation of p95 ≤ CT_target.
Phase-detection thresholds and CT_target values are stable and well-characterized under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions; no unknown environmental factors will invalidate threshold tuning.
open_risks: Real-time latency: Phase-detection accuracy and SafetyAlert delivery depend on timely transducer sampling and compute scheduling. If NGHE Solution lacks sufficient computational priority or real-time guarantees, cycle-phase detection may drift, invalidating cycle-time measurements and delaying safety alerts. Mitigation: Establish explicit latency SLAs (phase-detection <100 ms, SafetyAlert <50 ms) and allocate dedicated compute resources.

Sensor-mounting interface discipline: If Vehicle and NGHE Solution do not establish a clear boundary for transducer placement, wiring, power delivery, and mechanical integration, signal-processing logic may diffuse into Vehicle module or vice versa. Mitigation: Define and document sensor-mounting interface; NGHE Solution owns signal conditioning and aggregation; Vehicle owns only physical mounting and power.

Statistical analysis encapsulation: If p95 computation, variance analysis, and CT_target comparison logic are scattered across multiple code files or services, the DP<->IM bridge is weak and maintenance/testing becomes difficult. Mitigation: Encapsulate all statistical logic in a single, cohesive sub-module within NGHE Solution.

Dynamic parameterization: If operational context (terrain, load type, weather) requires frequent re-parameterization of CT_target or phase-detection thresholds, and the Tasking interface does not support runtime configuration, ad-hoc threshold tuning could leak into Vehicle or Safety Services. Mitigation: Tasking interface must support parameterized threshold updates with validation and atomic application.

SafetyAlert coordination: Alerts triggered by cycle-time exceedance must reach Safety Services and Vehicle in time to halt unsafe motion. If SafetyAlert latency is high or Vehicle does not respond promptly, safety plan compliance is at risk. Mitigation: SafetyAlert interface guarantees <50 ms delivery; Vehicle has hard real-time handler for motion-halt commands.

Acceptance test coverage: Verification of p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions requires careful test design (duration, sample size, environmental conditions, acceptance thresholds). If test plan is incomplete or does not cover edge cases, validation may be insufficient. Mitigation: Define comprehensive acceptance test plan with Telemetry data granularity sufficient for independent validation.
evidence_needed: Latency SLA specification: Document phase-detection latency <100 ms and SafetyAlert delivery <50 ms as explicit requirements in DP and IM boundary specification. Provide evidence of compute resource allocation and real-time scheduling guarantees within NGHE Solution.

Sensor-mounting interface specification: Define exact schema and ownership boundaries for transducer placement, wiring, power delivery, and mechanical integration. Provide evidence of interface agreement between NGHE Solution and Vehicle teams.

Statistical analysis sub-module design: Provide architecture diagram and code structure showing p95 computation, variance analysis, and CT_target comparison encapsulated in a single, cohesive sub-module. Demonstrate that statistical logic is not scattered across multiple files or services.

Tasking interface specification: Define exact schema and update frequency for CT_target and phase-detection threshold parameters. Specify whether updates are applied immediately, at cycle boundaries, or with a grace period. Provide evidence of parameter validation logic within NGHE Solution.
SafetyAlert interface specification: Define latency guarantees, delivery semantics, and Vehicle motion-halt handlers. Provide evidence of hard real-time implementation and safety plan integration.

Acceptance test plan: Define test duration, sample size, environmental conditions, and acceptance thresholds for p95 ≤ CT_target verification. Provide evidence of Telemetry data granularity sufficient for independent validation. Document test results under agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
